title: Musk vs. Altman: The $134 Billion Courtroom Showdown!
summary: Imagine you and a friend start a lemonade stand, promising to give away free lemonade to everyone. But later, your friend secretly turns it into a money-making business and keeps the cash. That's basically what Elon Musk says happened with OpenAI! Musk helped start OpenAI in 2015 as a nonprofit to build AI that helps all of humanity. But he claims CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman lied to him and later changed it into a for-profit company that could make billions. Now they're going to court in California. Musk wants a whopping $134 billion in damages—though he says the money should go to the nonprofit, not his own pocket. He also wants the judge to remove Altman and Brockman from their jobs. The trial will be wild: Musk, Altman, and even Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella are expected to testify. Secret texts and diary entries might be revealed! OpenAI says Musk is just jealous because his own AI company, xAI, is a competitor. The company is valued at over $850 billion and plans to go public soon. If Musk wins, it could completely upend the AI race. Nine regular people on the jury will give their opinion to help the judge decide. Stay tuned—this is going to be a bumpy ride!
why_it_matters: The outcome of this trial could decide who controls the powerful AI that might one day help with your schoolwork or answer your toughest questions, so it’s a fight over the future of learning.

keywords.explanation: An organization that uses its money to help a cause, not to make a profit.
A business that tries to earn money for its owners.
Money that a court orders someone to pay to fix a wrong they did.
To speak under oath in a court, telling the truth about what you know.
Chief Executive Officer, the top boss of a company.
A group of ordinary people who listen to a trial and help decide who is right.

options: Turned it from a nonprofit into a for-profit company
questions.correct_answer: Turned it from a nonprofit into a for-profit company

questions.question: How much money in damages is Musk asking for?

options: The president of the United States

options: He is secretly friends with Altman
He is jealous because his own AI company, xAI, is a competitor

questions.correct_answer: He is jealous because his own AI company, xAI, is a competitor

questions.question: How much is OpenAI valued at, according to the article?
Who will help the judge make the decision?
options: Nine regular people on the jury
questions.correct_answer: Nine regular people on the jury

background_read: OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, was founded to make safe AI for everyone. Sam Altman became its CEO and later changed it from a charity-like nonprofit to a business that can earn money.
Elon Musk, known for running Tesla and SpaceX, also started a competing AI company called xAI. He says Altman lied about OpenAI's mission.
Microsoft, led by CEO Satya Nadella, invested billions into OpenAI, which helped it grow quickly.
Article_Structure: WHO: Elon Musk, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and Satya Nadella are key people in the case; nine regular jurors will also weigh in.
WHAT: Musk says OpenAI was supposed to be a free-AI charity but secretly became a money-making business, and he wants $134 billion in damages.
WHERE: The trial is taking place in a courtroom in California, USA.
WHY: The case could change who runs OpenAI and how future AI companies are allowed to operate, affecting the whole tech world.

perspectives.description: Some people support Musk, believing that big AI should stay nonprofit so it never gets greedy and always helps everyone equally.
Others think Musk is being a sore loser; his own AI company xAI is behind, so he wants to slow down OpenAI’s success.
Legal experts see this as an important test: can founders of nonprofits stop them from switching to for-profit when there’s too much money at stake?
